scenes.name: Title
scenes.text: From answers to learning. [pause] A paradigm shift for the AI classroom.

scenes.name: Default
scenes.text: When students meet AI, the pattern is the same. [pause] Ask. Receive. Copy. Move on. [pause] The homework is done — but very little has happened in the student's mind.

scenes.name: Effort
scenes.text: Learning is not the product. [pause] Learning is the <emphasis>effort</emphasis> that produces it. [pause] Questioning, struggling, failing, trying again. That is where understanding is built.

scenes.name: Two modes
scenes.text: The same tool, two opposite effects. [pause] An answer machine bypasses thinking. [pause] A learning partner guides it. [pause] The technology is neutral. The pedagogy is not.

scenes.name: Shift
scenes.text: The shift is from <emphasis>product</emphasis> to <emphasis>process</emphasis>. [pause] From 'write me an essay' to 'help me develop my thinking'.

scenes.name: Examples
scenes.text: Two students study fractions. [pause] One copies the answer. The other says: do not give me the answer — guide me step by step. [pause] Same tool. Different learning.

scenes.name: Teacher role
scenes.text: The teacher's job is no longer to ban AI. [pause] It is to design the conditions under which AI deepens learning instead of bypassing it.

scenes.name: Practices
scenes.text: Three concrete practices. [pause] Teach AI as a process tool. Design assignments that value drafts and reasoning. Be explicit about the purpose of every task.
